title: Multiple?
description:
Given an integer $n$, the sequence is called good if its elements are from $[1, n]$ and all its non-empty subsequences (not necessarily continuous) have sums not divisible by $n$.

Calculate the number of good sequences of length $n-k$ modulo $998\,244\,353$.
input: The only line of input contains two integers $n$ and $k$ ($1 \le k \le n/4 < n < 998\,244\,353$).
output: Print one number --- the answer to the problem.
source: Camp, Petrozavodsk Programming Camp, Winter 2021, Day 5: Almost Retired Dandelion Contest, ICPC Camp Contest 2, Contest, Open Cup, 2020/2021 Season, Stage 11: Grand Prix of Nizhny Novgorod
